source.currency_note: Includes amendments through Ord. No. 5839 (2025) and Ord. No. 5834 (2025). Verify against Municode before relying on for a permit.

disclaimer: This is a structured restatement of cited law for navigation/search. It is NOT legal advice. The controlling text is the Maui County Code. Every row carries a section pin-cite; confirm at the source.

uses.use: Agriculture
Agricultural land conservation
Agricultural parks (per HRS ch. 171)
Animal and livestock raising, incl. animal feed lots and sales yards
Private agricultural parks
Minor utility facilities (as defined in 19.04.040)

Retention/restoration/rehabilitation/improvement of buildings, sites, or cultural landscapes of historical or archaeological significance (excludes commercial zipline/canopy/bungee, which need a conditional permit under ch. 19.40)
Solar energy facilities <15 acres, ≤35% of lot, compatible with existing ag uses (compatibility waived on LSB class D/E soils)
Composting and co-composting operations (subject to HRS ch. 205)

permitted_accessory.label: Permitted accessory uses (incidental/subordinate to a permitted principal use)

uses.use: Farm dwellings — Maui/Lānaʻi: 2 per lot (one ≤1,500 sf developable area); Molokai: 2 per lot (one ≤1,000 sf)

Farm labor dwelling — 1 per 5 acres; Maui requires meeting 2 of 3 criteria (≥$35,000 gross ag sales/yr for 2 yrs per dwelling; ag water rate certification; or approved farm plan). Molokai/Lānaʻi require both (a) and (b).
Commercial agricultural structures — max 2 per lot (subject to ch. 19.36B parking)
Storage, wholesale and distribution (barns, greenhouses, ag supply/product/irrigation-water storage, farmers' cooperatives, similar)
Processing of agricultural products (majority grown in the County)
Energy systems, small-scale
Small-scale animal-keeping
Animal hospitals and animal board facilities (Molokai: Molokai planning commission approval)
Riding academies (Molokai: planning commission approval)

Open land recreation (enumerated list: hiking, noncommercial camping, fishing, hunting, equestrian, rodeo arenas, arboretums, greenways, botanical gardens, guided ag/plantation tours, petting zoos, garden tours, hang gliding, paragliding, mountain biking, accessory restrooms). Uses not specifically permitted here, in 19.30A.060(A)(7), or ch. 19.40 are PROHIBITED.
Bed and breakfast homes (ch. 19.64; not on Molokai) — qualifying via bona fide ag operation ($35,000 gross sales/2 yrs), or small-lot/farm-plan criteria, or listed historic site
Short-term rental homes (ch. 19.65) with fully implemented farm plan consistent with HRS ch. 205
Parks for public use (no golf courses; no commercial uses unless gov-supervised)
Family child care homes (HRS 46-15.35(b); registered per HRS ch. 346) in a legally permitted farm dwelling
Agricultural tourism on a farm (registration with Planning required; 8am–6pm; must not interfere with principal farming; not on Molokai)
Other uses primarily supporting a permitted principal use (planning commission must find conforming to intent)

special_use_permit.label: Special uses (allowed only with a special use permit under 19.510.070; state special permit may also apply under HRS ch. 205 for parcels ≤15 acres)

uses.use: Additional farm dwellings beyond 19.30A.050(B)(1)
Farm labor dwellings that do not meet 19.30A.050(B)(2) criteria
Commercial agricultural structures not meeting chapter standards
Public/quasi-public institutions necessary for agricultural practices
Major utility facilities (as defined in 19.04.040)
Telecommunications and broadcasting antenna

Open land recreation not meeting 19.30A.050(B)(10) (e.g., commercial camping, gun/firing ranges, archery, skeet, paintball, skateboarding, rollerblading, playing fields, rappelling — not within 500 ft of a waterfall)
Cemeteries, crematories, and mausoleums
Churches and religious institutions
Mining and resource extraction
Landfills
Solar energy facilities greater than 15 acres
Home businesses — permitted with a State special permit (HRS 205-6); must comply with ch. 19.67 and obtain a County special use permit when ch. 19.67 requires
Airports, heliports, drive-in theaters, country clubs, drag strips, motor sports facilities, golf courses, golf driving ranges
On Molokai: commercial zipline, canopy, rappelling, and bungee jumping
Open land recreation uses/structures not specifically permitted by 19.30A.050(B)(10), 19.30A.060(A)(7), or ch. 19.40 (default-prohibited)

district_standards.max_developable_area: 10% of total lot area (applies to farm dwellings; not to ag-support structures e.g. storage, barns, silos, greenhouses, farm labor dwellings, stables, or permitted utility facilities)
district_standards.max_height: Dwelling 30 ft (vent pipes/fans/chimneys/antennae/solar on roofs ≤40 ft). Nondwelling structures (barn/silo) over 35 ft must set back 1 additional ft per ft of height.
district_standards.max_wall_height: 4 ft within yard setback (one utility wall per lot ≤7 ft H x 7 ft W; director of public works may allow greater for retaining earth/water for health/safety)
district_standards.subdivision: Maximum number of lots is keyed to gross lot acreage per the 19.30A.030(G) table; see also resubdivision limits at 19.30A.040.

related_provisions.topic: Definitions (active ag operation, ag food establishment, ag products stand, ag retail structure, commercial ag structure, farmer's market, producer, value-added)
Commercial agricultural structures (requirements, registration, size thresholds for special use permit)
Exemptions pursuant to State law (kuleana; affordable housing per HRS ch. 201E)
